input.counterSource: None found. The claim is fully supported by the primary source with no credible contradicting evidence identified.
input.evidence: The primary source (https://cloudscape.design/foundation/visual-foundation/content-density/) directly confirms every element of the claim. The page states verbatim: "Content density is defined by the ratio of information visible compared to the space available in the interface." It names exactly two modes: Comfortable ("the standard density level of the system, active by default") and Compact ("an additional density level for data intensive views"). The claim is a faithful restatement of the source quote, not an overreach. The source is a first-party AWS/Cloudscape design system specification — appropriate quality for this type of claim. No credible source contradicts this; the two-mode density pattern is consistent with Material Design, IBM Carbon, and other major systems. The documentation is current and actively maintained, not outdated. There is no marketing inflation — the page provides precise technical detail including the 4px base unit and specific spacing reduction behavior for compact mode.
